summary: Soundboard recording with good clarity captures a spiritually charged ’73 show highlighted by a scorching China > Rider, an epic Other One and a soaring Outer Limits jam.
review: The tape is a solid sound‑board source that sounds clear though a touch quieter than other ‘73 releases.  Fans rave about the magical first‑set “step‑back” intro to All Along the Watchtower and the second‑set fireworks – especially China > Rider, The Other One and the Outer Limits sequence with Ned Lagin – while a few feel the He’s Gone → Truckin’ transition falls flat.  Overall it’s an enjoyable, if uneven, snapshot of a memorable Dead night.

ai_rating.rationale: Average to above‑average musical performance (mixed reviews) combined with a good soundboard recording yields a solid mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear and well‑balanced, though some listeners note the overall volume is lower than typical ’73 tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts a bit subdued but builds with BE Woman and Big River; the second set rockets into space after He’s Gone, delivering long improvisations and memorable segues before a heartfelt encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Delivers a fine ‘BE Woman’, meanders on some jams but shines on China > Rider and The Other One.
band_member_comments.Keys: Ned Lagin’s synth adds an effective texture to the Outer Limits section.
